PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Johnson (via court call) for (administrator) John Hourihan.

The Court stated that this is a petition to terminate, that there is a problem in that there are still assets in the estate, that whatever can be accomplished by a 13100 should be, that an inventory & appraisal is needed and then a petition for final distribution. Mr. Johnson reported that there is $4100.00 in the estate, that they tried to e-file an inventory & appraisal and it bounced back, that they have resubmitted it and not heard anything and will file for final distribution. The Court took the petition to terminate estate off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Mar-07-2016 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re Filing Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition For Approval Of Status Report
Off Calendar Jan-11-2016 Continued To Mar-07-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the administrator John Hourihan and the Attorney Mark Johnson to appear on March 7, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the petition for final distribution or the petition for approval of status report has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re Filing Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition For Approval Of Status Report
Off Calendar Dec-28-2015 Continued To Jan-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Apr-27-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Johnson for John Hourihan.

Mr. Johnson stated that he was not aware that proof of the Testator's handwriting was required, provided case history, explained that stocks were in the Controller's office and expressed the difficulty in collecting the assets. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (order for the same signed in open Court). Bond is not required. The will was admitted into probate.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
